description: Fix the language gaps that make feature errors unshowable, then add a per-feature 'Rejected with' error example to the playground so goal's located, AI-ingestible diagnostics are on display.

userStories.title: Reject unknown enum variant construction
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need constructing an undeclared enum variant (e.g. `Light.Blue`) to be a located compile error so the enum's closed set is enforced at construction, not silently transpiled.
acceptanceCriteria: Running goalc on a file that constructs a variant not declared in its enum exits non-zero with a `file:line:col: error:` diagnostic that names the bad variant and lists the enum's valid variants.
The diagnostic carries a stable bracketed code (e.g. `[unknown-variant]`) distinct from the existing `[unknown-error-variant]` Result code.
A valid enum construction (e.g. `Light.Red`) still transpiles cleanly (goalc exits 0).
testdata/check/01-enums/ contains a positive case annotated with a `// want` marker and a negative (clean, no-marker) case, and the internal/sema test suite discovers and passes them.
The diagnostic is authored in internal/sema/*.goal and the committed generated *.go is regenerated (task check's verify-generated passes).
userStories.notes: New checker diagnostic. Construction (not match) is the gap: match on an unknown variant is already handled; Enum.NotAVariant currently transpiles silently to a phantom struct. If a corpus/manifest check-case count is asserted in a test (see internal/corpus/generate_test.go), bump it when the new testdata case lands.

userStories.title: Locate the question-outside-Result error
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need the error for using `?` in a function that cannot carry the error to be located (file:line:col) and coded, matching every other diagnostic, so the playground can show it with a source position.
acceptanceCriteria: Using `?` in a function whose return type is neither Result nor Option produces a located `file:line:col: error:` diagnostic (currently it is an unlocated `backend:` message).
The message names or references the enclosing function's non-fallible return so the fix is obvious.
The diagnostic carries a stable bracketed code.
Existing valid `?` programs (features/05-question-prop examples and any question-prop testdata) still transpile cleanly.
If the fix lives in goal source (internal/sema or internal/backend .goal), the committed generated *.go is regenerated so verify-generated passes.
userStories.notes: Today `goalc` prints `backend: ` + "`?` outside a Result- or Option-returning function" with no line:col. End state is a located, coded diagnostic; checker vs backend placement is an implementation choice, but US-003 renders it via the playground so it must carry a position.

userStories.title: Verify playground errors against the checker
userStories.description: As the playground build, I need error features to be verified against the checker (sema.Analyze), not only backend.Transpile, so exhaustiveness/must-use/implements/derive diagnostics can be locked and displayed.
acceptanceCriteria: cmd/build-playground's error-feature verification runs the checker on the example source and, when it produces an Error-severity diagnostic, asserts the rendered diagnostic equals the locked ```error``` block exactly (trailing newline trimmed).
The rendered diagnostic uses the feature's SourceName (e.g. `traffic.goal:9:2: error: ...`), not an absolute or placeholder path.
When the checker reports no error, verification falls back to the existing backend.Transpile assertion, so the current `unsafe-default` backend example still verifies unchanged.
A build-playground unit test covers an error feature whose expected output is a checker diagnostic (verification passes) and one whose locked text is wrong (verification fails).
`go run ./cmd/build-playground` still exits 0 on the current docs/by-example.md.
userStories.notes: Core enabler for all checker-backed authoring stories (US-006..US-009). No change to the authored '## Rejected with' doc format is required — only the verification source. Reuse sema.Analyze + Diagnostic.Render, the same path goalc's checker uses.

userStories.title: Add doctest-failure output kind
userStories.description: As a playground author, I need a distinct output kind for a failing doctest so a feature can show a wrong `>>>`/expected pair and its test failure, rather than a transpile rejection.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/byexample parses a doctest-failure output block (a labeled fence distinct from `Transpiles to`/`Rejected with`) and threads a new OutputKind value through the parsed record.
cmd/build-playground maps the new kind to a distinct expected-pane label (e.g. "test failure") in the generated features.json.
A byexample unit test asserts a feature section with the new block parses to the new OutputKind with the expected locked body.
Existing `go`, `test`, and `error` output kinds continue to parse unchanged (existing byexample tests pass).
userStories.notes: Parser + data-model only; the build-time verification of the failure is US-005. app.js consumes the pane label as data, so no front-end edit is expected.

userStories.title: Verify doctest-failure at build time
userStories.description: As the playground build, I need a doctest-failure feature to be proven real by compiling and running its generated test and asserting it fails, so the shown failure cannot drift from the toolchain.
acceptanceCriteria: cmd/build-playground verifies a doctest-failure feature by generating the doctest `_test.go` (as `goalc -test` does), compiling and running it, and asserting the run fails.
The locked failure block matches the observed test-failure output (the `got %v, want %v` message), trimmed, or a normalized subset the verifier documents.
A build-playground test covers a passing doctest (must NOT be accepted as a doctest-failure feature) and a failing doctest (accepted).
`go run ./cmd/build-playground` exits 0 with the new verification wired in and no doctest-failure features yet present.
userStories.notes: Runs go build/test on a generated file in a temp dir; keep IO behind the existing generator seams. Highest-risk story — split if compile+run cannot be finished and verified in one pass.

userStories.title: Author match and result error examples
userStories.description: As a playground visitor, I need to see the error goal emits for a non-exhaustive match and for a dropped Result, so the exhaustiveness and must-use guarantees are visible.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/by-example.md gains an error example under the match feature whose locked block is the live `[non-exhaustive-match]` diagnostic for a missing variant.
docs/by-example.md gains an error example under the Result (open-E) feature whose locked block is the live `[dropped-result]` diagnostic.
`go run ./cmd/build-playground` exits 0 (re-verifying both locked messages against the live checker).
site/features.json contains two `"outputKind": "error"` features whose `expected` fields contain `[non-exhaustive-match]` and `[dropped-result]` respectively.
userStories.notes: Snippets can be adapted from testdata/check/02-match and testdata/check/03-result. Depends on US-003.

userStories.title: Author closed-E and implements error examples
userStories.description: As a playground visitor, I need to see the error for an unknown closed-E variant (or missing from-conversion) and for a struct that fails its declared interface, so the closedness and implements guarantees are visible.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/by-example.md gains an error example under the Result (closed-E) feature whose locked block is a live closed-E diagnostic (`[unknown-error-variant]` or `[missing-from-conversion]`).
docs/by-example.md gains an error example under the implements feature whose locked block is a live `[unimplemented-method]` or `[method-signature-mismatch]` diagnostic.
`go run ./cmd/build-playground` exits 0.
site/features.json contains error features under the closed-E and implements categories whose `expected` fields contain the corresponding bracketed codes.
userStories.notes: Snippets adaptable from testdata/check/06-error-e and testdata/check/07-implements. Depends on US-003.

userStories.title: Author completeness, assert, derive examples
userStories.description: As a playground visitor, I need to see the errors for an incomplete struct literal, an always-false assert, and an unsourced/unbridged derive field, so the completeness, static-assert, and conversion-totality guarantees are visible.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/by-example.md gains an error example under the no-zero-value feature whose locked block is a live `[missing-field]` diagnostic (distinct from the existing `unsafe-default` backend example).
docs/by-example.md gains an error example under the assert feature whose locked block is a live `[assert-always-false]` diagnostic.
docs/by-example.md gains an error example under the derive-convert feature whose locked block is a live `[unsourced-field]` or `[unbridged-field]` diagnostic.
`go run ./cmd/build-playground` exits 0.
site/features.json contains error features whose `expected` fields contain `[missing-field]`, `[assert-always-false]`, and an `[unsourced-field]`/`[unbridged-field]` code.
userStories.notes: Snippets adaptable from testdata/check/08-no-zero-value, 10-assert, 12-derive-convert. Depends on US-003.

userStories.title: Author enum and question-prop examples
userStories.description: As a playground visitor, I need to see the error for constructing an unknown enum variant and for `?` in a non-fallible function, plus a note on why Option has no standalone error, so the enum and `?` guarantees are visible and Option's omission is justified.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/by-example.md gains an error example under the enums feature whose locked block is the live diagnostic added in US-001 for unknown-variant construction.
docs/by-example.md gains an error example under the `?` propagation feature whose locked block is the live located diagnostic from US-002.

docs/by-example.md documents, under the Option feature, why Option has no standalone 'Rejected with' example (its guarantee is structural: the value is unreachable without a match, and dropping an Option is legal absence).
`go run ./cmd/build-playground` exits 0.
site/features.json contains error features under the enums and `?` propagation categories.
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001, US-002, and US-003.

userStories.title: Author failing-doctest example
userStories.description: As a playground visitor, I need to see a doctest whose expected output is wrong and the resulting test failure, so the doctests guarantee (examples that cannot silently drift) is visible.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/by-example.md gains a doctest-failure example under the doctests feature: a goal snippet with a `>>>`/expected pair that does not match, plus its locked failure block.
`go run ./cmd/build-playground` exits 0 (the failure is verified by compiling and running the generated test per US-005).
site/features.json contains a feature under the doctests category with the doctest-failure output kind and its expected failure text.
